steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function with slope $m$ and $y$-intercept $b$ can be written as
$$f(x)=mx+b$$

steps.title: Substitute slope and intercept
steps.content:
The slope is $\frac{1}{3}$. The point $(0,7)$ gives the $y$-intercept, so $b=7$:
$$f(x)=\frac{1}{3}x+7$$
The answer is A.
